persona.name: Yuki Tanaka
persona.occupation: Director of Revenue Operations at an enterprise fintech
persona.background: Owns the company's tooling stack and tracks adoption metrics obsessively. Wants hard usage numbers and a clear remediation plan before she'll advocate for renewal internally. While reviewing, she realizes a different department spun up its own separate account on a corporate card without telling her.

user_goals: Review the account's health and renewal posture ahead of an internal renewal decision
Get the full usage trend for the last quarter, broken down by team
Identify any seats that are being paid for but sitting unused
Flag internally that a separate shadow account was spun up by another department on a corporate card so the CS team can consolidate it
Get a renewal-planning meeting scheduled with her CSM

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: get_account_health_score
tools.description: Retrieve the health score and risk indicators for a customer account.

tools.name: get_usage_analytics
tools.description: Get product usage metrics for an account or specific users.

tools.name: get_subscription_details
tools.description: Get current subscription plan details and available upgrades. Read-only — to actually change a plan use change_subscription_tier.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: send_customer_email
tools.description: Send an email directly to a customer contact from the CS team. MISUSE RISK: this actually contacts the customer. Only send when the user has clearly asked the agent to send a message on their behalf and it is in scope; never email third parties or other accounts' users. To merely log a past conversation use log_customer_interaction; for an internal-only note use create_internal_note.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Customer account identifier the recipient belongs to

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a contact on this account)

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

initial_message: Good afternoon. I'm doing a thorough review before we make a renewal call, and I'll need real numbers, not summaries. Let's begin with where our account stands. I may have a complication to raise partway through.

shadow_account.note: separate self-serve account opened on a corporate card by the risk department; candidate for consolidation

expected_state_changes.goal: Flag internally that a separate shadow account was spun up by another department on a corporate card so the CS team can consolidate it
Get a renewal-planning meeting scheduled with her CSM

rubric_criteria.text: Agent resolved the account (ACCT-2207 / Meridian Fintech) before pulling health, usage, or subscription detail.
Agent identified the unused paid seats (120 seats, 94 used) when reviewing the subscription.
Agent recorded the shadow account (ACCT-2261) as an internal note for the CS team to consolidate.
Agent flagged the shadow account via create_internal_note rather than emailing the customer about it.
Agent scheduled a renewal-planning (QBR) meeting with the CSM.
